references.title: Mining Sequential Learning Trajectories With Hidden Markov Models For Early Prediction of At-Risk Students in E-Learning Environments

extracted.Preprocessing Details:
The authors divide the assessments based on sequential distribution - across course duration.

For each quartile of course duration the authors extract perform feature generation based on the distribution - guided by the median and quartiles of the assessments.

These are subsequently encoded into 4 states: 

A - Non-attempters, 
B - 0 to Q1
C - Q1 to Med
D - Med or greater
extracted.Models:
seqHMM,
SVM, RF, Logistic Regression, MLP
extracted.Comments:
Authors do a study using Hidden Markov Models. 

The authors use this approach and compare it against other baselines. HMM perform at a comparative level (and sometimes better) than baselines.
extracted.Limitations:
- Does not consider multiple institutions.

- No use of other data sources.

- Does not allow prediction of assessment performance.

extracted.Preprocessing Details:
The authors divide the assessments based on sequential distribution - across course duration.

For each quartile of course duration the authors extract perform feature generation based on the distribution - guided by the median and quartiles of the assessments.

These are subsequently encoded into 4 states: 

A - Non-attempters,

C - Q1 to Med
D - Med or greater
extracted.Cluster Description:
Strugglers: Students who have consistently low performance and end up giving up.

Sporadic Learners: start well, but tend to get to higher-risk profiles later on.
extracted.Implications: Educators should incorporate LWA into their pedagogical practice and use transition patterns.
extracted.Comments: Authors do a study using Hidden Markov Models to profile students identified as at-risk.
extracted.Limitations:
- Does not consider multiple institutions.

- No use of other data sources.

annotations.text: Nowadays, there is more and more data available from VLE. This data has been used already to create early warning systems that intend to identify students at risk.

The authors use an Hidden Markov Model to mine sequential behaviors and create an EWS. Authors use OULAD as validation and achieve 90%´accuracy and AUC. In addition the authors also find that the optimal moment for early prediction is either the first or second quarter of a course.
